posts.cooked:
Hello everyone,
I have recently played LBA1, and i thought that having a whole LBA game in isometric is very clever. And i was wondering, could you modify LBA2 to replace all the exteriours with 2d isometric terrrain?
- Gobbles: HQR replacements are possible, but you would also need a new LBA 2/TO .EXE file for that, which we cant afford to, as we dont have the LBA 2/TO source code. Sorry.
But is it possible in anyway? hacking the file? it must be possible, but just not easy to do. Or could you reconstruct the game?

- Gobbles: hacking the .EXE file is not a good idea for such big things. I tried to hack the LBA 2 .EXE file, in order, to display texts RTL (right-to-left), but I didnt succeed, since for RTL, I needed extra lines to be put into the code, which cant be done by hacking the .EXE file, as you cant change its size, otherwise, it wont work. Sorry. But it is possible. Just we have to modify the scenes, redirecting some points into isometric scenes, instead of 3-D ones (I think there is some code in the scenes, which decides, whether to load a 3-D environment, or an isometric one). So, we should think about this solution

Inst possible.

Youll need to make the isometric island design, thing that we cant make it now and when could be possible will be an hard work. Other thing most hard is to modify the scenes.hqr. The 3D scenes will not match the 2D scenes and will need to be re-write.

Well if making an No CD crack its a little difficult in some cases, changing the way the Engine works will be impossible

- alexfont: if we preserve the original 3D positions in the isometric scene, then, the SCENE.HQR files would be OK, and they would just need a simple correction, in order to load isometric environment, instead of the 3D one. Actually, there would be no need to hack the .EXE files, nor to completely rewrite the scene files. So, its worth considering.
Reminds me of the weird Zelda:Ocarina of Time 2D project (oot2d), which vanished off the face of the earth.
Couldnt you also reconstruct it into the LBA1 engine? you could import all of the interiour scenes, and remake the 3d outdoors in 2d isometric?
- Gobbles: Its impossible, becuase in this way, you should convert all sprites, etc., since LBA 1/Relentless uses a different palette, or, just change the game palette.

I agree with OB - you just need to force the camera to view the scene isometrically like you can do in cinema 4D. There is no need to recreate the world.

The point of this exercise however eludes me somewhat.

I continue thinking this is an impossible/extremellyhard job to do.

Anyway I think scene files have a paramenter to distinguish the outside and inside scene in the zones. In this way will be necessary change all the zones that connect to the outside scenes. Will be necessary too, add the Grids and Layouts in the LBA_Bkg file, and this could be another problem. The LBA2 saved game in the holomap var game use bitfields to distinguish the outside and inside scenes, probably other var game made the same.

- alexfont:

I meant this: in all LBA 2 scene files, at the beginning, there is (I think) a bit, that determines, whether the scene is isometric (LBA_BKG.HQR) or 3-D (*.ILE). If its 3-D, then it also specifies the index of the island, which data it has to load (or multiple, if there are various option, like for Citadel Island and Celebration Island), and then, it specifies, which part of the island it has to load (again, with the index). Of course, with 3-D scenes, all other parts (of the same island) are also loaded, to display the whole 3-D island. We just have to know, how to hack the bits.
And you think this will resolve the problem? Just changed the scene?
- alexfont: who knows. Maybe yes, maybe not. Still its rather impossible to do, as we dont know that bit, so, also Gobbles, give up. Its too hard to be done.

Give what up? i didnt start anything ok, i was just pondering with ideas of mine that is all. Perhaps in the future it will be quite easilly possible, members are busily working on modifications forum.
the angry: The point of this is to experience LBA2 the way many players did with LBA1.

why not make a remake of the game, which checks if you have actually got an install of LBA2 (for legal reasons).

hacking the files would propably just be as hard as remaking it
THats what i was thinking, but you would have to remake the character models and animation, which is the hardest job of making a game.
But, cant you tell the camera to onlt view from 1 angle? It wouldnt be like lba1 then, becouse you can still see depht, but at least the camera is only filming from 1 corner

Yes you are making a good point Bot13, couldnt you turn the perspective off too? then it would be genuenly isometric. And is there a way to actually control the camera during the game? like to get an Areal view of a whole island without moving Twinsen places?

Gobbles:

Yes you are making a good point Bot13, couldnt you turn the perspective off too? then it would be genuenly isometric. And is there a way to actually control the camera during the game? like to get an Areal view of a whole island without moving Twinsen places?

yeah. a whole camera controller would be brilliant.
